commit.message:
Redesign to word-card view: handwriting crop + text per word

Complete overlay redesign. Each line shows word cards side-by-side
(right-to-left for Hebrew), where each card has:
- Top: cropped handwriting image of that word (using x-coordinates)
- Bottom: OCR text translation

Click a word card → fixed edit bar at bottom shows:
- The handwriting crop (so you see what was written)
- The original OCR detection text
- Previous correction if any
- Input to type the correct text

No more side-by-side layout, no more layout reflow on edit.
Removed unused highlight/lock state from old approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: PromptArena tool_result_has_media and tool_result_media_type assertions (#625) (#645)

Add two new eval handlers for asserting multimodal content in tool results:
- tool_result_has_media: checks that a tool result contains ContentPart
  entries with a specified media type (image, audio, video, document)
- tool_result_media_type: checks that a tool result contains non-text
  ContentPart entries with a specified MIME type

Both handlers inspect ToolCallRecord.Result (typed as any) by attempting
type assertion to []types.ContentPart, with fallback to map-based
extraction for JSON-deserialized results.

commit.message:
Add /ds:diagnose and /ds:ask-keystone commands (#1)

* Add /ds:diagnose and /ds:ask-keystone commands

Migrate two DS commands from thanx-ai workspaces to the canonical
plugin repo:

- /ds:diagnose: Analyzes partner integration emails, classifies
  integration type, queries Thanx docs, and generates structured
  integration guides with certification requirements
- /ds:ask-keystone: Researches Thanx platform questions using
  Keystone MCP tools, publishes answers to the Knowledge Gaps
  Notion database, and saves local backups

Bumps plugin version to 0.2.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix autonomy guardrails and .gitignore for new commands

- Add approval gate to /ds:ask-keystone Notion publish step:
  present proposed page details and wait for explicit user
  confirmation before creating, matching the pattern from
  /ds:triage and /ds:improve
- Add credential safety rule: never display NOTION_API_TOKEN
  or other secrets in conversation output
- Add .context/ to .gitignore (both new commands write output there)
- Wrap long lines in ask-keystone.md for readability
- Move Notion block format examples into fenced code block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace curl-based Notion publish with Notion MCP tools, add slug sanitization

Address Juliano's review feedback on PR #1:
- Replace the entire curl/raw API approach in ask-keystone Step 4 with
  Notion MCP tools (notion-search, notion-create-pages)
- Remove .env credential reading logic — MCP handles auth
- Add slug sanitization rules to both ask-keystone and diagnose commands
  (lowercase, hyphens, path-safe, 50 char limit)
- Renumber rules in both files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add fetch step to Notion publish workflow for schema discovery

notion-search returns the Knowledge Gaps database as a page type,
so the agent needs to fetch it first to get the data_source_id and
property schema before creating pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address CodeRabbit review: MCP failure handling, sources section, slug cap

- Add hard-stop behavior when thanx-docs MCP is unavailable: output
  "Blocked by docs retrieval" and fall back to Discovery Questionnaire
- Add Sources section to guide template for doc traceability
- Add 50-character max length to slug sanitization rule

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR #1 review feedback: harden slug sanitization, add failure handling, and security guardrails

- Add deterministic slug sanitization via sed commands in both commands
- Add MCP failure handling block to ask-keystone (matches diagnose pattern)
- Cap ask-keystone slug at 36 chars to account for YYYY-MM-DD- prefix
- Add SQL injection guardrail for replica_query usage
- Add PII redaction rule for persisted diagnosis files
- Add unknown-partner fallback slug when partner name is unclear
- Fix misleading "best match" question type wording
- Clarify fallback cross-reference in diagnose classification step
- Unify sanitization wording across both commands

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Juliano's remaining observations on diagnose and ask-keystone

- Add date to diagnose output filename ([slug]-YYYY-MM-DD.md) to prevent
  silent overwrites on repeated partner diagnoses
- Add Notion MCP failure handling in ask-keystone Step 4, matching the
  pattern already used in Step 2 for Keystone failures
- Cap Keystone exploration at 8-10 tool calls to prevent rate limit
  exhaustion or runaway sessions
- Inline LIMIT guidance at replica_query usage in Step 2 so agents see
  it before constructing queries (was only in Rule 6 before)
- Restructure diagnose Step 3 to short-circuit doc searches — overview
  first (covers auth + cert), then only search uncovered endpoints,
  capped at 8 total searches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
replace CI workflow with local-only watcher

Removed GitHub Actions workflow. All automation is local:
each machine runs ./scripts/watch-and-run.sh which polls
for new commits, pulls, launches a claude session, and pushes.

Machines skip their own commits (no loops). SSH remote enforced.
Stale lockfile detection. Push retry on conflict.

Usage:
  ./scripts/watch-and-run.sh          # continuous polling
  ONCE=1 ./scripts/watch-and-run.sh   # single session

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial release: multi-backend YOLO detection with model pooling and grid caching

Extracted from vision-intelligence monorepo as a standalone open-source package.

Features:
- YOLO-World, YOLOE, and standard YOLO backends via ultralytics
- Optional Perceptron API backend
- Thread-safe model pooling with queue-based acquire/release
- Grid-based detection caching for selective re-inference
- Crop-zoom-verify post-processing for false positive reduction
- Geometry utilities: IoU, dedup, area filter, polygon filter, proximity filter
- Configurable via environment variables with sensible defaults

commit.message:
fix: resolve request flooding, polling storms, and UI rendering issues

- useApi.js: add global concurrency limiter (MAX_CONCURRENT=6) with request
  queue and per-URL in-flight deduplication; remove 3x retry amplification
  that was causing browser connection exhaustion (ERR_ABORTED on all requests)
- Dashboard.jsx: increase polling intervals 3-10x across 18 hooks to prevent
  request storms; fix loading gate to not block forever on API errors
- api.js: add trailing slashes to signals/data-sources endpoints to prevent
  FastAPI 307 redirects that bypass Vite proxy
- useTradeExecution.js: increase polling from 2s to 10s (6 concurrent fetches
  every 2s was the worst flood source)
- AlignmentEngine.jsx: independent try/catch per endpoint for graceful
  degradation instead of fail-all-on-one-error
- TradeExecution.jsx: fix unicode escape rendering (\u00B7 → ·)
- MarketRegime.jsx: fix unicode rendering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(docs): setup de manutenibilidade com testes, semantica e tooling (#32) * docs: initialize project * chore: add project config * docs: add requirements, roadmap and state for v1 milestone Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * docs(01-manutenibilidade-setup): create phase 1 plans 3 parallel plans (all wave 1) covering MAINT-01..04, NAV-01, TEST-01: - 01-01: fix date bug in Layout.astro + unify desktop/mobile nav - 01-02: configure ESLint (TypeScript + Astro) + Prettier - 01-03: configure Vitest with React 19 + jsdom Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * fix(01-01): injetar EVENT_START_DATE via define:vars em Layout.astro - commit.message:
Fix: trigger Supabase upsert with .then() so changes actually persist

Supabase JS v2 queries are lazy PromiseLike — without .then() the HTTP
request never fires. Also move the upsert outside setState updater
(side effects in updaters are an anti-pattern).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Extract repeated UI patterns into reusable components

New components:
- PageHero: page-top hero section (used on 8 pages)
- SectionHeader: centered h2 + subtitle (used on 8 pages)
- CTASection: green gradient CTA banner (used on 9 pages)
- FeatureCard: icon + title + description card, centered or horizontal layout
- StatCard: large stat value + label + description
- StepCard: numbered step with slot for body content

All 9 pages updated to use the new components with no content changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Dashboard actualizado para usar Supabase directo

Problema: El dashboard se quedaba en blanco porque el hook
useSupabaseDashboard todavía hacía fetch a /api/compras que
había sido eliminada.

Solución: Actualizado useSupabaseDashboard para llamar a
Supabase directo, eliminando la API route intermedia.

Cambios:
- useSupabaseDashboard ahora usa supabase.from('compras').select('*')
- Eliminada dependencia de /api/compras en el dashboard
- 1 Edge Request menos por carga del dashboard

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add semantic search behavior documentation

Documents how semantic search works, HNSW index behavior, why broad
queries return fewer results, precision filter design, and trade-offs
vs keyword search.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Climate Predictor project and update README

- Full end-to-end climate analysis: 158 countries, 1950-2022
- EDA: CO2/temperature trends, sea level cointegration, heatmaps, distributions
- PCA + K-Means clustering (3 climate zones, 73.2% variance explained)
- Temperature anomaly analysis with Arctic amplification finding
- 5 model families: Linear, Polynomial, Random Forest, MLP, Ridge+Interaction
- Best model: Random Forest R²=0.35 (country-level)
- All 33 plots exported to Data/plots/ subfolders
- Updated README with full project description and model comparison table
- Added .gitignore for .claude/ and __pycache__

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand plugin from 10 to 22 skills, add 2 agents

Add 12 new skills:
- usgs-earthquakes: USGS ComCat induced seismicity data
- usgs-waterdata: USGS NWIS + Water Quality Portal
- fred-prices: FRED commodity pricing (WTI, Brent, Henry Hub)
- epa-ghg: EPA GHGRP facility-level GHG emissions (Subpart W)
- worldbank-energy: World Bank energy indicators by country
- comtrade-minerals: UN Comtrade Li/Mg international trade flows
- doe-geothermal: DOE GDR/OpenEI geothermal co-production data
- crossref-doi: CrossRef DOI resolution and citation metadata
- opec-data: OPEC production via EIA STEO API
- iea-open: IEA free datasets (EV tracker, prices, GHG, NZE, CCUS, SDG7)
- tnav-reservoir-sim: tNavigator-style simulation emulation (black oil, PVT, AHM, VFP, petrophysics)
- pnge-visual-explainer: Self-contained HTML visualizations for PNGE concepts

Add 2 new agents:
- pnge-geopolitics: Energy geopolitics and supply chain analysis
- pnge-gis-mapper: Interactive Leaflet.js map generation

Update li-mg-prospector to reference all 20 data skills and 3 companion agents.
Update README.md (10 → 22 skills, 1 → 3 agents, expanded usage examples).
Update TOKENS.md with FRED, OpenEI, Comtrade, EPA credential sections.

commit.message:
Fix: Round % Descuento to integer to eliminate WooCommerce tax rounding artifacts

Since discounts are always round percentages (10, 15, 20%), round() without
decimals gives the exact integer regardless of ex-tax precision loss.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Centralize CSS: move inline styles from HTML files into styles.css

Eliminates ~1,500 lines of duplicated inline <style> blocks from
calculator.html, rules.html, and index.html by consolidating all
shared and page-specific styles into styles.css. Replaces hardcoded
colors with CSS variables, fixes rules.html header to use span tag
for consistency, and adds missing nav-active class to rules nav.

global to ESLint config for the figma-codex directory.

Implements TDD Task 4: regex-based parser for .figma.tsx Code Connect files.
Extracts import style, component name, import source, Figma URL, file key,
node ID (dash-to-colon conversion), example JSX, and props mapping.
All 17 parser tests pass; full suite 222/222 green.

Implements the resolver that takes parsed ParsedFigmaFile data and
resolves the actual component source file to extract prop types,
sub-components, and classify components as simple/compound/composite.

Implements writeManifest() which serialises the resolved component map
into figma-codex.json with version, generatedAt timestamp, gitHash, and
collected figmaFiles metadata. Handles absolute vs relative output paths
and creates missing output directories automatically.

* feat(figma-codex): add npm scripts, exports, and generated manifest

- Add codex:generate and codex:watch scripts to package.json

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(figma-codex): regenerate manifest on .figma.tsx commit via lint-staged

* feat(figma-codex): update expected-output.json fixture with real snapshot

* docs(figma-codex): add README documentation

* docs(figma-codex): fix imports.primary format in README examples

* fix(figma-codex): handle multi-line function prop types in extractProps

Replace the regex-based prop parser with a balanced-bracket position scanner.
The old regex used ([^;\n]+) which truncated multi-line prop types at the first
newline, causing inner object fields (href, isActive, className) to leak as
top-level props. The new scanner tracks () and {} depth and only terminates a
type on ; or \n when bracket depth is 0, correctly capturing full function
signatures like renderLink's (props: {...}) => React.ReactNode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Panel de Control mobile + fix admin route permissions

- Panel de Control completo: announcements, users, store, signals, chat-mod, stats, schedule, live-schedule, students, roles, courses-admin, settings (13 screens)
- Profile screen: Panel de Control button visible only for TEACHER/ADMIN/CREATOR
- Backend: events/admin, courses/admin, signals/:id/close now use requireRole instead of requireLevel (accessible without MASTER membership)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix HD notifications parser for merged cells and label spacing

- Deduplicate values in read_row_text to prevent merged cells from
  repeating the same value (was causing issue_date to parse as NaT)
- normalize_label now strips spaces before colon (handles 'Issue Date :'
  and 'BL# :' variants with 0/2/4 spaces)
- Simplify HEADER_MAP to only the 4 needed fields: BL#, Vendor ID,
  Vendor Name, Issue Date
- Duplicate labels in a file now silently use first occurrence instead
  of raising ValueError

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: quality-copq-analytics
repository.description: git repo for copq

commit.message:
Enhance test deduplication tool with AI-powered consolidation and improved UX

Major features and improvements:
- Rebranded tool to "Cuda Test Consolidator" with centered header
- Integrated Claude 3.5 Haiku via IQ API for intelligent test analysis
- Added editable merged summary fields with localStorage persistence across pagination
- Implemented automatic status transition to "Rejected" for duplicate tests
- Added Jira issue linking and commenting for merged tests
- Enhanced 3-step wizard workflow with modern Bootstrap 5 UI
- Added 13 latest AI breakthrough facts to loading screen
- Improved form validation and error handling
- Added command-line arguments support (--port, --host, --debug)
- Removed obsolete main.py in favor of run.py

Technical changes:
- Added transition_issue() method to JiraClient for status updates
- Enhanced dedup_service with Claude 3.5 Haiku model configuration
- Implemented session-based selection clearing to prevent stale data
- Added similarity_percent property to CandidatePair model
- Improved textarea for merged summaries (3 rows, 300px min width)
- Updated IQ API token field with setup guide link

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

EN translation: AI Consulting → AI Audit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
